rulings.Case Title: 152 GEARY ST. LLC, VS. VIJAYA PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 14, 2023, line 1. Defendant's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel (Vijaya Properties, LLC) is OFF CALENDAR, insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and 1013. Per the proof of service on file, motion served on Vijaya Properties, LLC by mail on January 18, 2023, which does not provide for additional 5 days for notice. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL MANUEL CASTRO AND KATHERINE MAY CASTRO, VS. RICHARD M. PATEL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 14, 2023, line 2. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Adjudication is OFF CALENDAR. Motion was not served at least 105 days before March 13, 2023 trial date and noticed to be heard within 30 days of trial without leave of Court in violation of CCP 437c(a). Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: DAVID TSAO VS. GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Amended Answer
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 14, 2023, line 4. Defendant's Motion for Leave to File an Amended Answer is DENIED. Moving party appears to seek to "clarify" an affirmative defense already asserted in the answer and to assert an additional defense that is not ripe. In light of the fact that the original answer was filed almost 2 years ago, the original trial date on the first amended complaint was set for January 17, 2023 and that discovery is closed, the amendment is unnecessary and prejudicial. Any clarification issues regarding 22nd affirmative defenses shall be addressed with the trial judge. If the 29th proposed affirmative defense ripens before trial in this action, Defendant may either re-file a motion for leave to amend (time permitting) or raise the issue with the trial judge. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: HEIDI HOI Y. LAM ET AL VS. CHARLES BIRKNER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 14, 2023, line 6. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is GRANTED. Plaintiff failed to comply with 37.9A(f)(2). Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the propositions that a defective Memorandum may be retroactively cured; that a defective memorandum achieved the purpose of the statute at the time it was filed; or that substantial compliance is sufficient to properly withdraw the property from the rental market under the Ellis Act. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SHUXIAN FENG VS. JOSE LOPEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 14, 2023, line 7. Defendant's Motion to Strike Amended Complaint is DENIED. First Amended Complaint (subject to the demurrer and motion to strike heard on December 27, 2022) and the verification filed pursuant to the Court's December 27, 2022 rulings (i.e. "Plaintiff shall have 5 days from the notice of entry of order to file a compliant verification," and "from proper verification being filed and served") is the complete verified operative complaint in this action. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
